In summary, Agent2 concludes that `one-way ANOVA` should be used based on its ability to effectively analyze differences in mean peak flows among various smoking categories while avoiding overcomplication found in other potential choices. This conclusion emphasizes simplicity yet robustness aligned closely with standard practices in medical statistics when dealing with group-wise comparative analyses.
